title: Phenol/Chloroform Genomic DNA Extraction Protocol
author: Daniel J. Richter
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.inzcdf6
materials: In hood, shake vigorously to mix.
Precipitate several hours, or overnight at -20º C.

steps.title: In a centrifuge, spin cells down...
steps.instruction: In a centrifuge, spin cells down in a 50 mL conical tube at maximum speed for 20 minutes at 4º C. 00:20:00, Maximum speed Maximum speeds vary, adjust according to individual availability.

steps.title: Per cell pellet, add lysis buffer...
steps.instruction: Per cell pellet, add lysis buffer in multiples of 700 μL per pellet. undetermined For example, 1.4 mL, 2.1 mL, etc., depending on pellet volume. Lysis Buffer for Phenol/ chloroform genomic DNA extraction (makes 20 mL of buffer) by Daniel J. Richter

steps.title: Pass sample through a 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Pass sample through a 1 mL or a 5 mL syringe fitted with a 21 gauge needle, twenty times.

steps.title: Spin cells down again for 20...
steps.instruction: Spin cells down again for 20 minutes at maximum speed at 4º C, to pellet unlysed cellular material. 00:20:00 Maximum speeds vary, adjust according to individual availability.

steps.title: Split the supernatant from each conical...
steps.instruction: Split the supernatant from each conical tube into 700 μL aliquots and place into 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes. If desired, retain mostly bacterial unlysed pellets for later use.

steps.title: Add 3
steps.instruction: Add 3.5 μL RNAse A per tube (4 mg/ml, final concentration 20 ng/μl). undetermined, RNAse A

steps.title: Incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature on a nutator
steps.instruction: Incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature on a nutator. 00:05:00

steps.title: Incubate at 50º C for 3 hours, swirling occasionally
steps.instruction: Incubate at 50º C for 3 hours, swirling occasionally. 03:00:00

steps.title: Add 7 μL of Proteinase K...
steps.instruction: Add 7 μL of Proteinase K (at 10 mg/mL, final concentration of 100 ng/μL). undetermined, Proteinase K

steps.title: Cool to room temperature
steps.instruction: Cool to room temperature.

steps.title: Add 700 μL of phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol...
steps.instruction: Add 700 μL of phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol at a pH of 8.0, per tube. undetermined, per tube All phenol:chloroform steps should be performed in the chemical hood.

steps.title: In centrifuge, spin for 10 minutes...
steps.instruction: In centrifuge, spin for 10 minutes at maximum speed, at room temperature. 00:10:00

steps.title: Transfer aqueous phase (upper) into a new tube
steps.instruction: Transfer aqueous phase (upper) into a new tube.

steps.title: Repeat phenol:chloroform:isoamyl extraction (steps 11-14 above)...
steps.instruction: Repeat phenol:chloroform:isoamyl extraction (steps 11-14 above) until no protein remains at interphase. This can take 3-4 times.

steps.title: Add an equal volume of chloroform:isoamyl...
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (no phenol), and shake vigorously to mix.

steps.title: Spin for 10 minutes at maximum speed, at room temperature
steps.instruction: Spin for 10 minutes at maximum speed, at room temperature. 00:10:00

steps.title: Remove aqueous phase (upper), combining into...
steps.instruction: Remove aqueous phase (upper), combining into a single tube with room for 3X the volume of sample. Can use a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube or a 15 mL conical.

steps.title: Add 1/20 of the volume of...
steps.instruction: Add 1/20 of the volume of 10 M ammonium acetate, for a final concentration of 0.5 M.

steps.title: Add 1/3 μL of GlycoBlue per...
steps.instruction: Add 1/3 μL of GlycoBlue per 100 μL of aqueous phase.(at 15 mg/mL, final concentration 50 ng/μL)

steps.title: Add 2x the volume of 100% EtOH
steps.instruction: Add 2x the volume of 100% EtOH.

steps.title: Spin at maximum speed at room temperature for 20 minutes
steps.instruction: Spin at maximum speed at room temperature for 20 minutes. 00:20:00

steps.title: Pour off supernatant
steps.instruction: Pour off supernatant.

steps.title: Add 1 mL or 10 mL...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL or 10 mL of 100% EtOH to wash pellet. Volume of EtOH to use depends on whether the pellet is in a 1.5 mL or 15 mL tube.

steps.title: Wash on nutator for 5 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Wash on nutator for 5 minutes at room temperature. 00:05:00

steps.title: Spin 5-20 mintues at maximum speed
steps.instruction: Spin 5-20 mintues at maximum speed. 00:20:00

steps.title: Repeat 100% EtOH wash, for a total of 2-3 washes
steps.instruction: Repeat 100% EtOH wash, for a total of 2-3 washes.

steps.title: Pour off supernatant
steps.instruction: Pour off supernatant.

steps.title: Add 1 mL or 10 mL...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL or 10 mL of 70% EtOH, depending on tube size, to wash pellet.

steps.title: Wash on nutator for 5 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Wash on nutator for 5 minutes at room temperature. 00:05:00

steps.title: Spin 5-20 minutes at maximum speed
steps.instruction: Spin 5-20 minutes at maximum speed. 00:20:00

steps.title: Remove supernatant, dry pellet for 5-10...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant, dry pellet for 5-10 minutes, do not over dry. 00:10:00

steps.title: Re-suspend overnight in a small volume...
steps.instruction: Re-suspend overnight in a small volume (~50-500 μL) of DNAse-free water.

steps.title: Pour off supernatant
steps.instruction: Pour off supernatant.

steps.title: Repeat 70% EtOH wash for a total of 2-3 washes
steps.instruction: Repeat 70% EtOH wash for a total of 2-3 washes. Volume of EtOH to use depends on whether the pellet is in a 1.5 mL or 15 mL tube.
